weeks.topic: A Family Member You Are Proud Of

questions.question_text:
Describe a family member who you are proud of. You should say:
who this person is
how you know them
what they do or have done
and explain why you find them memorable or important

chunks.text: To begin with, I want to talk about my uncle,
who is an accomplished (有造诣的 / 成功的) doctor,
because I truly look up to (敬仰) him.
Actually, he is a very diligent (勤奋的) person
who always puts others first.
Whenever his patients need help,
he is considerate (体贴的) and completely selfless (无私的).
In fact, he has a generous (慷慨的) nature
and essentially has a heart of gold (心地善良).
For example, he will always go the extra mile (加倍努力)
to ensure (确保) everyone is safe.
I think, seeing his devoted (挚爱的 / 忠诚的) attitude
is highly inspiring (鼓舞人心的) to me.
To be honest, I felt so proud when he saved a life last year.

questions.question_text:
Describe a teacher who has helped you learn something important. You should say:
who this person is
how you know them
what they do or have done
and explain why you find them memorable or important

chunks.text: I want to talk about my high school math teacher, Mr. Chen.
To begin with, he was one of the most inspiring (鼓舞人心的) teachers I have ever had.
Honestly, he always used simple examples to explain difficult ideas,
which made me feel that math was actually enjoyable.
He was very diligent (勤奋的) and prepared every lesson with great care.
For example, he would always go the extra mile (加倍努力) by staying after class
to help students who were struggling.
I think he was also very selfless (无私的) because he never asked for anything in return.
In the end, I learned that hard work really matters,
and this lesson changed how I study everything.
He is truly an accomplished (有造诣的 / 成功的) teacher.
He is special to me because he believed in me
when I did not believe in myself.

questions.question_text:
Describe a neighbor or family friend who is a good person. You should say:
who this person is
how you know them
what they do or have done
and explain why you find them memorable or important

chunks.text: I'd like to talk about my neighbor, Mr. Chen.
Honestly, I have known him for about ten years,
since I was a child.
He lives just next door to my family.
To begin with, he is a very considerate (体贴的) person.
For example, he always helps elderly neighbors carry heavy bags.
He is also very generous (慷慨的) and often buys food and small gifts for people around him.
Actually, he is so devoted (挚爱的 / 忠诚的) to his community
that he volunteers every weekend,
which shows how much he cares about others.
In the end, I truly look up to (敬仰) him
because he never asks for anything in return.
I think he has a heart of gold (心地善良).
To be honest, knowing him has taught me
that small, kind actions can make a big difference in people's lives.

questions.question_text: What would children do to make their parents proud?

chunks.text: Generally speaking, children can make their parents proud
by excelling in their education,
as academic success is highly valued.
This is because, parents often sacrifice (牺牲) time
to instill (灌输 (价值观)) good study habits in their kids.
For instance, when a student achieves top grades,
parents truly appreciate (感激 / 欣赏) the immense effort shown.

questions.question_text: What did you do to make your parents proud when you were young?

chunks.text: Looking back, I made my parents proud
when I decided to follow in someone's footsteps (继承衣钵 / 效法) by learning the piano.
The primary reason is that, my mother is a musician
who wanted me to share her passion.
As an example, performing well at my first recital (独奏会)
proved that the apple doesn't fall far from the tree (有其父必有其子).

questions.question_text: What advantages of yours make you proud?

chunks.text: From my perspective (观点 / 视角), I am most proud of my ability to maintain peace,
because it creates harmony (和谐) in my social circles.
This is largely due to the fact that,
resolving disputes (争端) requires immense patience and excellent communication skills.
For example, whenever my classmates argue,
I step in to offer calm guidance (指导) to resolve the issue.

questions.question_text: When was the last time that you felt proud of yourself?

chunks.text: To be specific, I felt incredibly proud last month
when I helped bridge a generation gap (代沟) between my grandfather and me.
This was significant because, younger people and older relatives
often struggle to comprehend (理解) each other.
As an illustration, I taught him how to use a smartphone,
which greatly improved our daily conversations.

questions.question_text: How does family pride contribute to the stability of a society?

chunks.text: It is widely acknowledged that, family pride creates a strong foundation,
which ultimately leads to a more stable community.
The core reason is that, people naturally want to protect and support
their own flesh and blood (骨肉 / 亲人).
For instance, families that respect each other are far less likely
to commit crimes (犯罪) in public areas.

questions.question_text: Do you think the definition of "success" within families has changed between the past and the present?

chunks.text: Undeniably, the definition of success has evolved (演变),
as modern families value personal happiness over just financial wealth.
In the past, success simply meant being the main breadwinner (养家糊口的人)
to provide basic necessities (必需品).
Today, however, achieving a healthy work-life balance
and pursuing individual passions are seen as equally important.

questions.question_text:
Describe a foreign country where you'd like to work for a short time. You should say:
where this place is
when you visited or first went there
what you did or saw there
and explain why this place is special to you

chunks.text: To be honest, I would love to work in London,
which is a cosmopolitan (世界性的 / 国际化的) city,
because it offers many excellent opportunities.
Personally, I want to immerse (沉浸) myself in the culture
and avoid any culture shock (文化冲击).
Hopefully, I can find a lucrative (利润丰厚的 / 赚钱的) job in the technology sector soon.
I think, working there will genuinely help me broaden one's horizons (开阔眼界).
Of course, I am an adaptable (适应性强的) and diligent (勤奋的) person,
so I will easily overcome any language barrier (障碍).
Even if, I have to start from scratch (白手起家 / 从头开始),
it will give me a new perspective (视角) on life.

questions.question_text:
Describe a country you would like to visit for a holiday. You should say:
where this place is
when you visited or first went there
what you did or saw there
and explain why this place is special to you

chunks.text: I would love to visit Japan for a holiday.
To begin with, Japan is a very cosmopolitan (世界性的 / 国际化的) country
where people from all over the world come together.
Actually, I want to immerse (沉浸) myself in Japanese culture
by visiting old temples and trying local food.
Honestly, I first heard about Japan from a friend
who worked there in a lucrative (利润丰厚的 / 赚钱的) job
and told me amazing things about the people and cities.
I think I am quite adaptable (适应性强的),
so I am not afraid of new places.
Of course, I know I might feel some culture shock (文化冲击) at first
because everything will be so different,
which means I will need time to get used to it.
In the end, Japan feels like a dream place for me to explore.

questions.question_text:
Describe a place you visited where you learned something about a different culture. You should say:
where this place is
when you visited or first went there
what you did or saw there
and explain why this place is special to you

chunks.text: I want to talk about my trip to Japan,
which I visited two years ago during a school holiday.
To begin with, the place I went to was Kyoto,
a city full of old temples and traditional streets.
Honestly, I was amazed by how diligent (勤奋的) the local people were —
every shop and street was clean and well looked after.
For example, I learned that Japanese people work very hard
and always try to do their best, even in small things.
Actually, language was a big barrier (障碍) at first,
but the people were still very kind and helpful.
I think this experience helped me to broaden one's horizons (开阔眼界)
because I saw life from a completely new perspective (视角),
which made me want to start from scratch (白手起家 / 从头开始)
with my own attitude toward work and daily life.

questions.question_text: What kind of job is easy to get in a foreign country?

chunks.text: Generally speaking, jobs in the restaurant sector are the easiest to secure,
as they require fewer formal qualifications.
This is because, these entry-level roles usually welcome a highly diverse workforce.
For example, international students often find quick work in local cafes
to bypass the initial language barrier (语言障碍).

questions.question_text: Should young people work abroad?

chunks.text: In my view, young people should definitely try to work overseas,
because it provides an incredibly inspiring (鼓舞人心的) life experience.
The main reason is that, international travel fosters rapid personal growth
and deepens cultural integration (融合 / 一体化).
For instance, living in a foreign melting pot (大熔炉 (多种文化融合)) teaches young professionals
how to become independent quickly.

questions.question_text: Do Chinese parents encourage their children to work abroad?

chunks.text: Admittedly, many Chinese parents do encourage working abroad,
as they believe it leads to a more prosperous (繁荣的) future.
This is due to the fact that,
global experience can significantly enhance (提升) a resume in today's job market.
As an illustration, parents often support their children financially
so they can benefit from ongoing globalization (全球化).

questions.question_text: Why do some people not want to work overseas?

chunks.text: On the other hand, some individuals avoid working abroad,
because they fear facing isolation or racial discrimination (歧视).
Furthermore, leaving behind family and friends can ruin their sense of emotional stability
and social harmony (和谐).
For example, many people strongly prefer the quiet comfort of their hometown
rather than adapting (适应) overseas.

questions.question_text: What are the benefits of working abroad?

chunks.text: Without a doubt, working abroad offers immense benefits,
as it helps individuals develop vital cross-cultural communication skills.
This is because, employees must learn local customs,
following the idea that when in Rome, do as the Romans do (入乡随俗).
Consequently, this cultural flexibility makes them highly valuable
to international corporations (大公司) in the long run.

questions.question_text: How does the migration of skilled workers (brain drain) affect the economy of the country they leave?

chunks.text: Unfortunately, the phenomenon of brain drain (人才流失) severely impacts a developing nation,
because it removes essential talent.
The primary issue is that, losing highly educated professionals
slows down domestic innovation and technological progress.
For instance, when top engineers migrate (迁移) to wealthier countries,
their home nation struggles to build modern infrastructure (基础设施).

questions.question_text:
Describe your perfect job. You should say:
what it is
how you got it or learned about it
how you use it or why it matters
and explain why it is important to you

chunks.text: To begin with, my dream job (理想的工作) is to become an architect,
because I am highly adaptable (适应力强的), and I love designing things.
Actually, I first heard about this exciting profession from a documentary on television.
To get this job, I need to become fully qualified (合格的 / 胜任的) by studying advanced mathematics.
I know, it is a very demanding (要求高的 / 费力的) and challenging (充满挑战的) field to enter.
However, I want to climb the career ladder (在职业阶梯上晋升) and work for a prestigious (有声望的) company.
Ultimately, it is perfect because it is both a lucrative (赚钱的) way to make a living (谋生)
and incredibly fulfilling (令人满足的).

questions.question_text:
Describe a useful skill you would like to learn in the future. You should say:
what it is
how you got it or learned about it
how you use it or why it matters
and explain why it is important to you

chunks.text: I would love to learn data analysis in the future.
To begin with, this skill is very useful in many jobs today.
I would learn it by taking online courses
and watching video tutorials on YouTube.
Honestly, it is quite challenging (充满挑战的) to understand at first,
but I think I can do it with practice.
Of course, working with data is also very demanding (要求高的 / 费力的),
so I would need to study hard every day.
I think this skill is important
because many prestigious (有声望的) companies look for people
who can read and understand data well.
Furthermore, having this skill would help me climb the career ladder (在职业阶梯上晋升) much faster.
In the end, I believe doing this kind of work
would be very fulfilling (令人满足的)
because I enjoy solving problems
and helping my team make better decisions.

questions.question_text:
Describe an important goal you hope to achieve in the next few years. You should say:
what it is
how you got it or learned about it
how you use it or why it matters
and explain why it is important to you

chunks.text: One of my most important goals is to become a software engineer.
Honestly, this is my dream job (理想的工作) because I love working with computers every day.
To begin with, I want to become fully qualified (合格的 / 胜任的) by finishing my university degree
and getting some extra certificates.
Furthermore, I plan to do an internship,
which means I can get real work experience,
which will help me find a good job faster.
Of course, software engineering is a very lucrative (赚钱的) job,
so I can make a living (谋生) comfortably and also help my family.
I also want to be adaptable (适应力强的) because technology changes very quickly.
In the end, when I achieve this goal, I will feel very proud and happy.
I think I will feel that all my hard work was worth it.

questions.question_text: What jobs do children want to do when they grow up?

chunks.text: Generally speaking, children often dream of becoming doctors or astronauts,
as these roles seem highly heroic (英雄的).
This is largely because, kids are heavily influenced by the media
and the exciting cartoons they watch.
For instance, watching a superhero movie might spark the initial motivation (动力 / 动机)
to save lives when they are older.

questions.question_text: What should people consider when choosing jobs?

chunks.text: In my view, individuals must consider their personal interests,
because passion is essential (必要的) for long-term career satisfaction.
Furthermore, evaluating the level of job security (工作保障) is absolutely crucial
in today's unpredictable global economy.
For example, choosing a stable industry prevents the constant terrible fear
of sudden unemployment (失业) during a financial crisis.

questions.question_text: Is salary the main reason people choose a certain job?

chunks.text: Admittedly, salary is a major factor,
as everyone needs sufficient income to bring home the bacon (养家糊口).
However, a high salary alone cannot compensate (补偿) for a toxic work environment
or extreme daily stress.
Consequently, many employees will actually accept a slightly lower wage
if it guarantees a better work-life balance (工作与生活的平衡).

questions.question_text: What kind of jobs are well-paid?

chunks.text: It is widely acknowledged that,
roles in technology and finance are highly lucrative (赚钱的),
because they strictly drive the modern economy.
The core reason is that,
these sectors require specialized knowledge and professionals
who can think outside the box (跳出框框思考).
For instance, top software developers in prosperous (繁荣的) cities
receive massive financial bonuses for creating innovative programs.

questions.question_text: Do people's ideal jobs change as they grow up?

chunks.text: Undeniably, career aspirations (抱负) shift drastically over time,
as adults gain a much more realistic understanding of the world.
As people mature, they realize they do not want
to be stuck forever in a boring dead-end job (没前途的工作).
As an illustration, a teenager might want to be a singer,
but later cautiously chooses a stable accounting career instead.

questions.question_text: To what extent does technology (such as AI) threaten traditional job roles in modern society?

chunks.text: Without a doubt, the rise of automation (自动化) poses a significant threat,
as machines can now perform repetitive tasks perfectly.
The primary issue is that, this technological shift rapidly accelerates the brain drain (人才流失)
in traditional manufacturing sectors.
For example, factory workers are frequently replaced by intelligent robots,
forcing them to learn completely new digital skills.

questions.question_text:
Describe a movie you watched recently that you felt disappointed about. You should say:
when this happened
where it took place
what happened during it
and explain how you felt about this experience

chunks.text: To be honest, I want to talk about a sci-fi movie I saw last weekend,
because it was highly anticipated (期待已久的), and I love the actors.
Actually, the studio tried to hype up (炒作 / 吹捧) the film with amazing trailers.
However, the storyline was completely predictable (可预测的 / 老套的) and deeply confusing (令人困惑的).
I think, the acting was very mediocre (平庸的),
making the whole experience a huge let-down (令人失望的事).
Personally, I felt it was incredibly overrated (被高估的) by the critics.
In fact, watching it felt like a complete waste of time (浪费时间)
instead of being fulfilling (令人满足的).
Ultimately, it failed to meet my demanding (要求高的 / 费力的) expectations for a good film.

questions.question_text:
Describe a live event or show you attended that was not as good as you expected. You should say:
when this happened
where it took place
what happened during it
and explain how you felt about this experience

chunks.text: I want to talk about a movie screening I went to last year.
To be honest, it was one of the most anticipated (期待已久的) films of the year,
and I bought tickets weeks before it came out.
Actually, the cinema was in the city centre,
and I went with two close friends on a Friday night.
I had seen many good reviews online,
so I really thought it would be amazing.
However, the story was very predictable (可预测的 / 老套的),
which made me feel bored,
and the ending left me with nothing to think about.
Furthermore, the acting was quite mediocre (平庸的).
Honestly, it was totally overrated (被高估的).
In the end, the whole experience was a real let-down (令人失望的事).
I think I will read more careful reviews before choosing a film next time.

questions.question_text:
Describe a product you bought online that you were not happy with. You should say:
what it is
how you got it or learned about it
how you use it or why it matters
and explain why it is important to you

chunks.text: I want to talk about a movie ticket I bought online last year.
To be honest, I got it from a popular app called Taobao.
Actually, I chose this movie because many people online started to hype up (炒作 / 吹捧) it,
saying it was amazing.
Of course, I felt very excited before watching it.
However, the story was really confusing (令人困惑的)
because I could not understand what was happening,
which meant I felt bored for most of the film.
To begin with, I thought it would be a fulfilling (令人满足的) experience.
Honestly, it was not.
The plot was too demanding (要求高的) for a casual viewer like me.
In the end, I felt it was a complete waste of time (浪费时间).
I will check reviews more carefully before buying tickets online again.

questions.question_text: Do you believe movie reviews?

chunks.text: Generally speaking, I only trust reviews from independent critics,
as mainstream websites are often paid for marketing.
This is because, studios use their massive financial influence (影响)
to artificially boost the ratings of a blockbuster (大片 (卖座电影)).
For instance, many highly rated action films
turn out to be completely boring when you actually watch them.

questions.question_text: What are the different types of films in your country?

chunks.text: In my observation, audiences in my country
enjoy a wide variety of films,
though comedies are particularly beloved.
The primary reason is that, people seek lighthearted entertainment
as a motivation (动机) to relax after working hard.
As an example, the romance and martial arts genre (体裁 / 类型)
also attract massive crowds during national holidays.

questions.question_text: Are historical films popular in your country? Why?

chunks.text: Undeniably, historical films are extremely popular,
because they deeply connect the younger generation with their cultural heritage.
This is largely due to the fact that,
these movies often feature stunning traditional cinematography (电影摄影技术) and epic battles.
For example, historical stories are frequently a massive box office hit (票房大卖)
during the lively Spring Festival season.

questions.question_text: Do you think films with famous actors or actresses are more likely to become successful?

chunks.text: Admittedly, having a star-studded cast (众星云集的阵容) guarantees initial ticket sales,
as loyal fans will always support their favorite celebrities.
However, fame alone cannot save a movie
if the script is poorly written or the directing is weak.
Consequently, relying entirely on famous faces instead of good storytelling
is a very risky strategy for modern filmmakers.

questions.question_text: Why are Japanese animated films so popular?

chunks.text: It is widely acknowledged that, Japanese animation is globally successful,
because it explores deep philosophical themes beautifully.
Furthermore, their unique visual style easily keeps audiences completely on the edge of one's seat (由于紧张兴奋而坐立不安).
For instance, the smart combination of emotional storytelling and modern digital automation (自动化 (used in context of CGI))
creates true visual masterpieces.

questions.question_text: Should directors pay a lot of money to famous actors?

chunks.text: In my view, directors should not overpay actors,
as this practice severely limits the budget for actual production quality.
The core issue is that, excessive salaries take away funds
needed for proper editing and navigating strict censorship (审查制度) requirements.
Therefore, it is much wiser to invest money in talented script writers
rather than just paying expensive celebrities.

questions.question_text:
Describe a person who encouraged you to protect nature. You should say:
who this person is
how you know them
what they do or have done
and explain why you find them memorable or important

chunks.text: To begin with, I want to talk about my uncle,
who is a very conscientious (尽责的 / 有良心的) person,
because he constantly teaches me to be eco-friendly (环保的).
Actually, he is a truly passionate (热情的) advocate (提倡者) for sustainable (可持续的) living in our hometown.
For example, he always tries to do his bit (尽自己的一份力) by picking up litter in the park.
I think, he is a genuine nature lover (热爱大自然的人)
who makes sure to practice what you preach (言行一致) every day.
To be honest, his daily dedication is incredibly inspiring (鼓舞人心的) to me.
Therefore, his amazing actions are never predictable (可预测的) and always motivate me.

questions.question_text:
Describe someone you know who lives a very healthy or natural lifestyle. You should say:
who this person is
how you know them
what they do or have done
and explain why you find them memorable or important

chunks.text: I'd like to talk about my university friend, Li Wei.
Honestly, she is one of the most conscientious (尽责的 / 有良心的) people I know
when it comes to taking care of the environment.
To begin with, she always brings her own bags to the shop
and avoids buying plastic products.
She also eats mostly vegetables
and tries to buy food from local farms.
Actually, she became passionate (热情的) about this lifestyle
after watching a documentary about pollution,
which really changed how she thought about everyday choices.
Furthermore, she uses only eco-friendly (环保的) cleaning products at home.
I think she truly believes in living a sustainable (可持续的) life in every small way.
In the end, watching her made me want to do one's bit (尽自己的一份力) too,
so I started recycling more and using less plastic every day.

questions.question_text:
Describe a person who does volunteer work to help others or the community. You should say:
who this person is
how you know them
what they do or have done
and explain why you find them memorable or important

chunks.text: I want to talk about my friend Li Wei,
who is a strong advocate (提倡者) for protecting our local environment.
Honestly, she is a true nature lover (热爱大自然的人)
who spends her weekends cleaning up parks and planting trees near our neighbourhood.
To begin with, she joined a local green volunteer group about two years ago.
Actually, she goes out every Saturday,
so her work is quite regular and not predictable (可预测的) in a boring way —
she always finds new places that need help.
For example, last month she organised a river clean-up with over thirty people.
I think she is really inspiring (鼓舞人心的)
because she always practice what you preach (言行一致),
which means other people around her start caring more about nature too.
In the end, volunteers like her make our community cleaner and healthier.

questions.question_text: How do people protect the environment?

chunks.text: Generally speaking, people protect the environment by recycling daily,
because this massively reduces unnecessary waste.
This is absolutely crucial since, protecting local biodiversity (生物多样性) is necessary for human survival.
For instance, even though one person's effort feels like a drop in the ocean (沧海一粟),
collective community action makes a huge difference.

questions.question_text: Do you think it's important to protect wild animals/nature?

chunks.text: Undeniably, it is vital to protect nature,
as wildlife strictly maintains our fragile ecological balance.
The primary reason is that, failing to prioritize conservation (保护 (自然))
will destroy future food sources.
Consequently, we must bridge the generation gap (代沟)
to ensure older and younger people work together on this urgent issue.

questions.question_text: What are the main environmental problems in your country?

chunks.text: In my view, the most serious problem is severe air pollution (污染),
which deeply affects public health.
This is largely due to the fact that,
rapid industrial growth has prioritized profits over clean air.
As an illustration, heavy smog in major cities
is just the tip of the iceberg (冰山一角) regarding the damage.

questions.question_text: Do you think we should use more renewable energy? Why?

chunks.text: Without a doubt, we must aggressively shift to renewable (可再生的) energy,
because fossil fuels are rapidly depleting.
This is absolutely necessary since, traditional dirty energy sources
actively wreak havoc (造成严重破坏) on the global climate.
For example, utilizing solar panels can significantly reduce our carbon footprint
and prevent further deadly temperature increases.

questions.question_text: Should schools teach students about environmental protection?

chunks.text: Absolutely, schools must teach environmental protection,
as children are the future leaders of our planet.
The core reason is that, early education effectively fosters
a lifelong sense of responsibility towards nature.
For instance, teaching students about animal extinction (灭绝)
helps them genuinely understand the real-world consequences of human negligence.

questions.question_text: [New] How effective is international cooperation in solving global environmental crises?

chunks.text: It is widely acknowledged that, international cooperation is highly effective,
because pollution does not respect national borders.
This essentially means that, a single country's domestic policies cannot solve global warming alone.
Therefore, global treaties have a massive influence (影响)
on successfully reducing carbon emissions across the entire industrialized world.

questions.question_text:
Describe a place with a lot of trees that you would like to visit (e.g., a forest, oasis). You should say:
where this place is
when you visited or first went there
what you did or saw there
and explain why this place is special to you

chunks.text: To begin with, I really want to visit a tranquil (宁静的) bamboo forest in Sichuan,
because I need to get away from it all (远离尘嚣), which sounds amazing.
Actually, I discovered this picturesque (风景如画的) location while reading an eco-friendly (环保的) travel blog online.
Apparently, the scenery is completely breathtaking (惊人的 / 极美的) with endless lush (郁郁葱葱的) green trees.
I think, it is quite isolated (偏远的 / 孤立的), making it the perfect place to find some peace and quiet (宁静).
Furthermore, the local village focuses heavily on sustainable (可持续的) tourism.
Ultimately, I want to visit this place because it is totally off the beaten track (人迹罕至的).

questions.question_text:
Describe a river, lake, or beach that you enjoy visiting. You should say:
where this place is
when you visited or first went there
what you did or saw there
and explain why this place is special to you

chunks.text: I'd like to talk about West Lake in Hangzhou,
which is a really beautiful place close to where I live.
To be honest, I go there about once a month,
usually on weekends.
Actually, the area around the lake is very lush (郁郁葱葱的),
with tall green trees everywhere.
To begin with, I usually walk along the paths and take photos
because the views are honestly breathtaking (惊人的 / 极美的).
The lake looks very picturesque (风景如画的) in every season.
I think it feels quite tranquil (宁静的),
which helps me relax after a busy week at school.
Furthermore, there is one small path near the water
that feels completely off the beaten track (人迹罕至的),
so it is always quiet there.
I enjoy visiting West Lake because it is so peaceful,
which means I always feel much better when I leave.

questions.question_text:
Describe an outdoor area in your city where people go to relax. You should say:
who this person is
how you know them
what they do or have done
and explain why you find them memorable or important

chunks.text: I want to talk about a big park near the centre of my city.
It is not isolated (偏远的 / 孤立的), so it is easy for people to get there by bus or by bike.
Actually, the park has a lot of tall trees and a small lake,
which makes it feel very natural.
To be honest, people go there to walk, have a picnic,
or just sit and enjoy the peace and quiet (宁静).
Furthermore, the park uses eco-friendly (环保的) bins and solar-powered lights,
and the local government is trying to keep it sustainable (可持续的) for future visitors.
I think this place is popular because people living in the city
really want to get away from it all (远离尘嚣) after a busy week.
In the end, it is a simple but very nice place to relax.

questions.question_text: Why do people like visiting places with trees or forests?

chunks.text: Generally speaking, people visit forests to escape stress,
as modern life is often overwhelming.
This is due to the fact that, rapid urbanization (城市化) creates noisy and crowded living conditions.
For example, taking a walk among trees is literally a breath of fresh air (令人耳目一新的人或事) for exhausted city workers.

questions.question_text: Are natural views better than city views?

chunks.text: In my view, natural views are far superior,
because they provide immense psychological benefits.
The primary reason is that, staring at a concrete jungle (钢筋水泥森林 (城市))
all day increases mental fatigue.
As an illustration, looking at green plants
has a proven therapeutic (有治疗作用的 / 治愈的) effect on the human brain.

questions.question_text: Do all people need some nature?

chunks.text: Absolutely, every human needs access to nature,
as we are biologically connected to the earth.
This is crucial since, humanity evolved in a natural habitat (栖息地)
long before modern cities existed.
Consequently, getting back to nature (回归自然) on weekends
is essential for maintaining our emotional health.

questions.question_text: Are people hard-wired to protect the environment?

chunks.text: Admittedly, not everyone is naturally protective,
because greed often overrides environmental concerns.
The core issue is that, massive corporations frequently prioritize profits
over the preservation (保护 / 保存) of natural resources.
For instance, ongoing deforestation (森林砍伐) for farming proves
that many people value money over ecological balance.

questions.question_text: How does rapid urbanization impact the mental health of city dwellers?

chunks.text: Undeniably, city expansion heavily impacts mental health,
as it separates people from calming green spaces.
Furthermore, the constant traffic noise and severe pollution (污染)
contribute to very high stress levels.
Therefore, city residents frequently suffer from anxiety
compared to those living in quiet country areas.

questions.question_text: Should governments prioritize economic development over the preservation of natural green spaces?

chunks.text: It is widely acknowledged that, governments must balance development and nature,
because destroying green spaces is permanent.
This is necessary since, economic growth means nothing
if it destroys local biodiversity (生物多样性).
For example, building a factory over a park
permanently ruins the community's quality of life.

questions.question_text:
Describe an application (app) you usually use on your phone or computer. You should say:
what it is
how you got it or learned about it
how you use it or why it matters
and explain why it is important to you

chunks.text: To begin with, I want to describe WeChat,
which is truly an indispensable (不可或缺的) app,
because I use it daily.
Actually, this innovative (创新的) platform puts everything right at your fingertips (触手可及).
For instance, I use it to chat with friends,
ensuring I never feel isolated (孤立的).
I think, its clean interface is incredibly user-friendly (用户友好的 / 易用的)
and highly efficient (高效的).
However, the short videos can be quite addictive (令人上瘾的)
and I often lose track of time (忘记时间).
Sometimes, I am completely glued to the screen (盯着屏幕看)
watching breathtaking (惊人的) travel clips.
Overall, I feel that this app has completely transformed my social life.

questions.question_text:
Describe a website you use often for studying or work. You should say:
what it is
how you got it or learned about it
how you use it or why it matters
and explain why it is important to you

chunks.text: Actually, the website I want to talk about is Duolingo,
which is a language learning app I use almost every day.
To begin with, I use it mainly to practice my English
and improve my grammar skills.
Honestly, it has become truly indispensable (不可或缺的) to me
because I cannot imagine studying without it now.
The design is very user-friendly (用户友好的 / 易用的),
so even beginners can start easily.
Furthermore, I think the lessons are quite innovative (创新的)
because they use games and fun challenges to teach new words.
To be honest, the app is a little addictive (令人上瘾的),
which means I sometimes find myself completely glued to the screen (盯着屏幕看)
for over an hour.
I think this website is helpful to me
because it makes learning feel like playing,
which keeps me motivated every single day.

questions.question_text:
Describe a piece of technology that has changed your daily routine. You should say:
when this happened
where it took place
what happened during it
and explain how you felt about this experience

chunks.text: I want to talk about a music app called Spotify.
Honestly, I started using it about three years ago
when a friend told me about it.
To begin with, it changed my morning routine completely
because I used to spend a lot of time looking for songs.
Now, music is at your fingertips (触手可及),
which means I can start my day much faster.
Actually, the app is very efficient (高效的)
and helps me find new songs easily.
For example, I sometimes lose track of time (忘记时间)
when I listen to music before sleeping.
Furthermore, the number of songs available is truly breathtaking (惊人的).
To be honest, there is one small problem —
I sometimes feel a little isolated (孤立的)
because I wear headphones too much and ignore people around me.
In the end, I think this change has been mostly good for my daily life.

questions.question_text: What is the difference between how young and old people use apps?

chunks.text: Generally speaking, young people use apps for entertainment,
while older generations focus on practical tools.
This is because, teenagers have a much higher dependency (依赖) on social media
for their daily communication.
For instance, a young person might act like a social butterfly (交际花) online,
whereas grandparents only use banking apps.

questions.question_text: Why do some people dislike using apps?

chunks.text: In my view, some individuals dislike apps,
because they feel overwhelmed by constant daily notifications.
The primary reason is that, continuous phone alerts
lead to severe information overload (信息过载) and mental fatigue.
Furthermore, many older users are deeply concerned
that these applications secretly steal their personal privacy (隐私).

questions.question_text: What is the most popular app in your country?

chunks.text: Without a doubt, the most popular app is WeChat,
as it completely dominates digital communication.
This is largely due to the fact that,
the rapid digitalization (数字化) of society requires an all-in-one platform.
For example, it facilitates everything from daily business interaction (互动)
to paying utility bills with a single tap.

questions.question_text: Are people becoming too dependent on mobile apps?

chunks.text: Admittedly, society has become overly dependent,
because apps now control almost every daily task.
This reliance is a double-edged sword (双刃剑),
as it brings great convenience but severely reduces basic survival skills.
As an illustration, due to modern urbanization (城市化),
many young people cannot navigate their own city without maps.

questions.question_text: To what extent do mobile applications compromise user privacy and data security?

chunks.text: Unfortunately, mobile apps heavily compromise data security,
as many platforms legally sell user information.
The core issue is that, greedy companies track our location
and browsing habits to generate targeted advertising.
Therefore, poor cybersecurity (网络安全) measures often leave individuals completely vulnerable
to identity theft and financial fraud.

questions.question_text: Will digital tools eventually replace traditional face-to-face services entirely?

chunks.text: It is widely acknowledged that, digital tools will not replace humans,
because physical contact remains vital.
This is necessary since, humans naturally crave empathy
and real emotional connection during difficult or stressful situations.
For instance, speaking to a real doctor provides a therapeutic (有治疗作用的) comfort
that an AI robot simply cannot replicate.

questions.question_text:
Describe a time when you gave advice to others. You should say:
when this happened
where it took place
what happened during it
and explain how you felt about this experience

chunks.text: To begin with, I want to talk about giving constructive (建设性的) advice to my friend,
because he was very hesitant (犹豫的).
Actually, he was reluctant (不情愿的) to study for his IELTS exam.
Since we were in the same boat (处境相同),
I decided to share a word of wisdom (至理名言) with him.
I told him that, creating an efficient (高效的) schedule
is truly indispensable (不可或缺的) for success.
Luckily, he didn't get angry and really decided to take it to heart (往心里去 (认真对待)).
I think, he realized my suggestion was very sensible (明智的).
Ultimately, he was extremely grateful (感激的) when he finally passed his test.

questions.question_text:
Describe a time when someone gave you helpful advice. You should say:
who this person is
how you know them
what they do or have done
and explain why you find them memorable or important

chunks.text: Actually, I want to talk about a time
my older sister gave me some really helpful advice
during my university years.
To be honest, I was thinking about dropping out
because I was struggling with my studies and felt very stressed.
She sat down with me and gave me some very constructive (建设性的) feedback
about how to manage my time better.
At first, I was reluctant (不情愿的) to listen
because I thought she didn't understand my situation.
However, she told me that many students feel in the same boat (处境相同),
which made me feel less alone.
Her advice was very sensible (明智的) because it was simple and easy to follow,
which helped me stay focused and finish my degree.
In the end, I am truly grateful (感激的) for her kind words.
She really changed my life.

questions.question_text:
Describe a difficult decision you had to make. You should say:
when this happened
where it took place
what happened during it
and explain how you felt about this experience

chunks.text: I'd like to talk about my decision
to leave my hometown and study in a big city.
Honestly, I was very hesitant (犹豫的) at first
because I didn't know anyone there.
To begin with, the main problem was choosing
between a safe, comfortable life at home
and a better future somewhere new.
Actually, my mother gave me a word of wisdom (至理名言)
— she said good chances don't wait for you.
I really took it to heart (往心里去 (认真对待))
and started thinking more clearly.
Furthermore, my teacher told me that being efficient (高效的)
in making decisions is indispensable (不可或缺的) for young people
who want to grow,
which made me feel that waiting too long was just wasting time.
In the end, I chose to go,
and I felt both scared and excited.
Of course, I never regretted it.

questions.question_text: Should people prepare before giving advice?

chunks.text: Generally speaking, people should definitely prepare before giving advice,
as words can deeply impact others.
This is because, offering an objective (客观的) perspective requires careful thought
and understanding of the situation.
For instance, speaking without preparation often leads to careless comments
rather than true wisdom (智慧).

questions.question_text: Is it good to ask for advice from strangers online?

chunks.text: In my view, asking strangers online is highly risky,
because you cannot verify their actual expertise.
The primary reason is that, trusting anonymous forums
is often like the blind leading the blind (问道于盲).
Furthermore, sharing detailed personal problems on the internet
severely compromises your own digital privacy (隐私).

questions.question_text: What are the personalities of people whose job is to give advice to others?

chunks.text: Without a doubt, professionals who give advice
possess high emotional intelligence,
as their job requires empathy.
This is crucial since,
providing effective counseling (咨询 / 辅导) demands immense patience
and active listening skills.
As an illustration, a good therapist
always maintains a calm and supportive attitude
during every client interaction (互动).

questions.question_text: What are the problems if you ask too many people for advice?

chunks.text: Admittedly, asking too many people causes total confusion,
because everyone will offer a completely different opinion.
The core issue is that, conflicting advice creates a mental dilemma (进退两难的困境)
and paralyzes your decision-making.
Consequently, you might end up deciding to just sit on the fence (保持中立 / 观望) forever without acting.

questions.question_text: Is the advice given by older generations still relevant to the problems young people face today?

chunks.text: Undeniably, advice from older generations remains highly relevant,
as human nature does not change over time.
Even though technology advances rapidly,
the fundamental struggles of human relationships and careers are always the same.
Therefore, listening to grandparents prevents young people
from having to learn the hard way (通过吃亏学到教训).

questions.question_text: To what extent does professional counseling differ from advice given by friends or family?

chunks.text: It is widely acknowledged that, professional advice is strictly evidence-based,
whereas friends offer purely emotional support.
This is necessary since, trained experts use proven psychological strategies
for proper intervention (干预) in serious crises.
For example, a family member might just agree with you,
but a professional will challenge your thoughts.

questions.question_text:
Describe a child who enjoys drawing/painting. You should say:
who this person is
how you know them
what they do or have done
and explain why you find them memorable or important

chunks.text: To begin with, I want to talk about my nephew,
who is a very imaginative (富于想象力的) child,
because he loves painting.
Actually, he is incredibly gifted (有天赋的)
and clearly possesses a hidden talent (隐藏的天赋) for visual arts.
Whenever he holds a brush, he is truly in one's element (如鱼得水)
and becomes highly expressive (表现力强的).
In fact, he is very observant (观察力敏锐的)
and likes to think outside the box (跳出框框思考).
Even though, his bedroom gets very messy (凌乱的),
his parents are never reluctant (不情愿的) to buy him paint.
I think, he enjoys drawing because it allows him to share his feelings.
Ultimately, I am grateful (感激的) to see his creativity grow daily.

questions.question_text:
Describe a young person who is good at playing a musical instrument. You should say:
who this person is
how you know them
what they do or have done
and explain why you find them memorable or important

chunks.text: I want to talk about my cousin, Lisa.
She is only fourteen years old,
but she plays the piano really well.
To begin with, she started learning when she was just five years old.
Her parents took her to a local music school,
and she practised every day at home.
Honestly, I think she is very gifted (有天赋的)
because she can learn new songs very quickly.
Furthermore, her playing is so expressive (表现力强的)
that people always feel emotional when they listen to her.
She is also very observant (观察力敏锐的),
and she notices small details in music that other children miss.
I think she is quite imaginative (富于想象力的) too,
because she sometimes creates her own simple melodies.
In the end, she can truly think outside the box (跳出框框思考),
which makes her performances very special and memorable.

questions.question_text:
Describe a child you know who is very active and full of energy. You should say:
who this person is
how you know them
what they do or have done
and explain why you find them memorable or important

chunks.text: I'd like to talk about my little cousin, Mia.
She is seven years old and full of energy every single day.
To begin with, she loves drawing and painting more than anything else.
Honestly, her room is always messy (凌乱的) because she leaves her art supplies everywhere.
Around other children, she is very friendly and always wants to show them her latest drawings.
Actually, she was reluctant (不情愿的) to share her work at first,
but now she feels very confident.
I think she has a hidden talent (隐藏的天赋) because her paintings look amazing for her age.
When she is drawing, she is truly in one's element (如鱼得水 (在自己擅长的领域)),
completely happy and focused.
In the end, I feel so grateful (感激的) to have her in my life
because she reminds me to enjoy small things,
which makes every day feel brighter.

questions.question_text: Why do people choose to visit galleries and museums in person instead of viewing art online?

chunks.text: Generally speaking, people visit galleries in person,
because seeing a physical masterpiece (杰作) is much more impactful.
This is due to the fact that,
computer screens cannot accurately display the true aesthetic (审美的 / 美学的) texture of oil paint.
For example, standing in front of a real painting
allows for a much deeper emotional appreciation (欣赏 / 鉴赏) of the artwork.

questions.question_text: Should galleries and museums be free of charge to the public?

chunks.text: In my view, museums should absolutely be free,
as art must be accessible to everyone in society.
The primary reason is that, charging high ticket prices
excludes poor families from gaining cultural wisdom (智慧).
As an illustration, free public galleries help to paint a picture (描绘) of history
for children who cannot afford private lessons.

questions.question_text: Why do some people find life inspiration from visiting these places?

chunks.text: Without a doubt, exhibitions provide immense inspiration (灵感),
because they showcase the highest limits of human creativity.
This is crucial since, viewing historical artifacts gives people
a highly objective (客观的) perspective on their own modern struggles.
Consequently, an exhausted worker might feel highly motivated
after seeing how ancient people built beautiful statues from nothing.

questions.question_text: Why do most children draw more often than adults do?

chunks.text: Admittedly, children draw constantly to express themselves,
as they have fewer language skills than grown adults.
Furthermore, adults are usually more down to earth (务实的)
and busy with stressful jobs to sit and paint.
For instance, a child will freely draw a purple dog,
proving that beauty is in the eye of the beholder (情人眼里出西施)
without caring about rules.

questions.question_text: Is art education in schools as important as science or mathematics?

chunks.text: Undeniably, art education is equally important,
because it actively develops a student's creative problem-solving skills.
While math teaches strict logic,
art teaches kids how to invent new ideas
and express complex emotions properly.
Therefore, ignoring the arts creates robotic students
who lack the innovative thinking needed for future societal progress.

questions.question_text: How does engaging in artistic activities contribute to the cognitive development of a society?

chunks.text: It is widely acknowledged that, artistic activities boost cognitive (认知的) development,
as they challenge the brain to think visually.
This essentially means that, communities that support the arts
tend to produce more forward-thinking engineers and designers.
For example, learning to play musical instruments improves memory and spatial awareness,
making the whole society much smarter.

questions.question_text:
Describe an interesting building you'd like to visit. You should say:
where this place is
when you visited or first went there
what you did or saw there
and explain why this place is special to you

chunks.text: To begin with, I really want to visit a magnificent (宏伟的) museum in Beijing,
because I love history.
Actually, this iconic (标志性的) building is a famous landmark (地标)
that will definitely stand the test of time (经受住时间的考验).
I heard that, the inside is extremely spacious (宽敞的)
and filled with very imaginative (富于想象力的) artworks.
Even though, the exterior looks slightly dilapidated (破旧的),
it holds many contemporary (当代的) exhibits.
If I go there, I will be very observant (观察力敏锐的) to catch every tiny detail.
I think, seeing it in person will totally take one's breath away (令人叹为观止).

questions.question_text:
Describe a shop or market you enjoy visiting. You should say:
where this place is
when you visited or first went there
what you did or saw there
and explain why this place is special to you

chunks.text: I'd like to talk about a big shopping mall
called SKY Mall in the centre of my city.
To begin with, it is a very iconic (标志性的) building that many people know.
Honestly, it has become a landmark (地标) in our city
because so many tourists come to take photos there.
The building looks really magnificent (宏伟的),
with tall glass walls and bright lights at night.
Inside, it feels very spacious (宽敞的),
so I never feel crowded when I walk around.
Actually, the design is quite contemporary (当代的),
which makes it feel modern and exciting.
You can buy clothes, food, electronics, and many other things there.
I think I enjoy visiting this place
because the environment is comfortable and clean,
which makes shopping feel like a fun day out
rather than a boring task.

questions.question_text:
Describe an old building or place in your hometown that has special meaning. You should say:
where this place is
when you visited or first went there
what you did or saw there
and explain why this place is special to you

chunks.text: I'd like to talk about an old temple in my hometown.
To begin with, it is about 300 years old, so it has a very long history.
Honestly, some parts look a little dilapidated (破旧的 / 年久失修的),
but people still love visiting it.
Actually, the wall paintings inside are really imaginative (富于想象力的),
showing stories from ancient times.
For example, if you are observant (观察力敏锐的),
you will notice small details in every corner.
In the end, I think this temple has truly managed to stand the test of time (经受住时间的考验)
because it still brings people together for festivals and prayers,
which means it remains an important part of daily life.
Furthermore, the view from the front gate can really take one's breath away (令人叹为观止).
It is a place everyone in my hometown feels proud of.

questions.question_text: Should all museums and historical buildings sell tickets?

chunks.text: Generally speaking, museums should sell tickets,
as they need funds to maintain their beautiful heritage (遗产 / 传统).
This is because, the daily preservation (保护 / 维护) of ancient artifacts
requires a massive financial budget.
For instance, using ticket money helps hire experts
to restore an old painting so it remains a true masterpiece (杰作).

questions.question_text: Which kind of building do people prefer, villas or apartments?

chunks.text: In my view, wealthy people prefer villas,
while most standard citizens live in high-rise apartments.
The primary reason is that, a city skyscraper (摩天大楼)
provides affordable housing for thousands of daily workers.
As an illustration, living in a crowded residential (居住的 / 住宅的) block
is the only way to get a roof over one's head (栖身之处) in Beijing.

questions.question_text: Why do people prefer to live in new houses?

chunks.text: Without a doubt, people prefer new houses,
because they offer modern comfort and better safety features.
This is largely due to the fact that,
newly built homes have highly upgraded infrastructure (基础设施) like smart heating.
For example, older properties often have leaking pipes and broken windows,
which are extremely expensive to fix.

questions.question_text: Which do people prefer, modern buildings or traditional buildings?

chunks.text: Admittedly, young people prefer modern designs,
whereas the older generation deeply values traditional architecture.
The core issue is that, modern offices are practical,
but ancient temples have a unique aesthetic (美学的) charm.
Consequently, modern glass towers turn the city into a cold concrete jungle (钢筋水泥森林),
which many older citizens strongly dislike.

questions.question_text: How does the architecture of a city influence the cultural identity of its residents?

chunks.text: Undeniably, architecture shapes identity,
as local buildings constantly remind people of their shared history.
When a city protects its ancient walls,
it helps to successfully build bridges (建立关系 / 沟通) between the past and present.
Therefore, growing up around traditional courtyards
makes citizens feel incredibly proud of their unique local culture.

questions.question_text: Is it necessary to preserve old buildings even if they impede modern urban development?

chunks.text: It is widely acknowledged that, preserving old buildings is essential,
because history cannot be replaced once destroyed.
This means that, developers should carefully build around historical sites
rather than tearing them down for quick money.
For instance, European cities successfully mix ancient churches with new train stations
without losing their charm.

questions.question_text:
Describe a technology product you would like to own. You should say:
what it is
how you got it or learned about it
how you use it or why it matters
and explain why it is important to you

chunks.text: To begin with, I really want to buy a cutting-edge (尖端的 / 前沿的) laptop,
because my current computer is completely obsolete (过时的 / 淘汰的),
and I urgently need an upgrade.
Actually, this new device is a highly versatile (多功能的) tool that is absolutely perfect for school.
Even though it is quite compact (紧凑的 / 小巧的),
the internal hard drive is incredibly spacious (宽敞的).
I know that it is extremely pricey (昂贵的)
and will definitely cost an arm and a leg (极其昂贵).
However, the magnificent (宏伟的) screen makes it a definite must-have (必备品) for my daily homework.
Ultimately, owning this state-of-the-art (最先进的) machine would make my study sessions much more productive.

questions.question_text:
Describe a gift you received that uses modern technology. You should say:
when this happened
where it took place
what happened during it
and explain how you felt about this experience

chunks.text: Actually, the gift I want to talk about
is a wireless earphone set
that my older brother gave me for my birthday last year.
To be honest, I was really surprised
because it is quite pricey (昂贵的 (informal))
and I never expected something so nice.
To begin with, the earphones use cutting-edge (尖端的 / 前沿的) noise-cancelling technology,
which makes them perfect for studying in noisy places.
Furthermore, they are very compact (紧凑的 / 小巧的),
so I carry them in my bag every single day.
I think they are also very versatile (多功能的)
because I use them for listening to music,
joining online classes, and making phone calls.
In the end, these earphones have truly become a must-have (必备品) in my daily life,
and they remind me how much my brother cares about me,
which always makes me feel warm inside.

questions.question_text:
Describe an old item you own that still works well. You should say:
what it is
how you got it or learned about it
how you use it or why it matters
and explain why it is important to you

chunks.text: I want to talk about my old laptop.
Honestly, I have had it for about seven years now.
To begin with, many people think it is obsolete (过时的 / 淘汰的) because newer models look much better.
Actually, I use it every day for studying and watching videos online.
The screen is still magnificent (宏伟的) and very clear.
Furthermore, it has a spacious (宽敞的 (storage)) hard drive,
so I can save all my files easily.
I think the reason I have not replaced it is simple —
new state-of-the-art (最先进的) laptops cost an arm and a leg (极其昂贵),
which means I cannot afford one right now.
To be honest, my old laptop still runs well
because I take good care of it,
and it does everything I need.
In the end, I feel there is no real reason to change it yet.

questions.question_text: What tech products are your friends using these days?

chunks.text: Generally speaking, my friends are currently obsessed with Virtual (虚拟的) reality headsets,
because they completely Revolutionize (彻底变革) modern gaming.
This is because, these immersive devices allow young players
to experience entirely new worlds right from their bedrooms.
For example, instead of a boring Distraction (干扰 / 分心),
they cleverly use it to explore ancient historical Heritage (遗产) sites online.

questions.question_text: Why do people love using tech products?

chunks.text: In my view, people genuinely love modern gadgets,
as they provide massive convenience and endless entertainment options.
The primary reason is that, heavy tasks that previously took hours
can now be completed at the click of a button (弹指之间 / 极其便捷).
As an illustration, we can instantly order hot food
or seamlessly book taxis without ever leaving our comfortable homes.

questions.question_text: What are the differences between chatting online and chatting face-to-face?

chunks.text: Undeniably, face-to-face communication builds deeper emotional bonds,
whereas texting often causes unfortunate interpersonal misunderstandings.
This is largely due to the fact that,
digital messages completely lack essential physical body language and vocal tone.
Consequently, relying solely on internet chatting frequently leads to
a severe sense of social Isolation (孤立) among teenagers.

questions.question_text: Has technology widened the economic gap between the rich and the poor?

chunks.text: Admittedly, technology has heavily widened the wealth gap,
as expensive devices are strictly limited to affluent (富裕的) families.
However, a clear Silver lining (一线希望 / 坏事中的好事) is that cheap smartphones
now give poor students excellent access to free education.
For instance, upgrading digital Infrastructure (基础设施) in rural villages
helps impoverished (贫困的) children finally learn critical skills online.

questions.question_text: Do you think people are losing their ability to communicate effectively due to screens?

chunks.text: Without a doubt, heavy screen usage severely damages our social skills,
because we practice real conversations much less frequently.
This is primarily since, younger generations constantly text rather than speaking directly
just to Keep up with the times (与时俱进).
Therefore, many teenagers feel extremely awkward and painfully anxious
when they have to order food face-to-face.

questions.question_text: To what extent should governments regulate the development of Artificial Intelligence?

chunks.text: It is widely acknowledged that, governments must strictly regulate Artificial Intelligence (人工智能),
as it possesses highly dangerous capabilities.
The core issue is that, unregulated algorithms (算法) could easily manipulate (操纵) public opinions
or steal private user data.
For example, creating strict international laws effectively prevents wealthy tech companies
from secretly abusing these powerful new computer systems.

questions.question_text:
Describe an occasion when you lost your way. You should say:
when this happened
where it took place
what happened during it
and explain how you felt about this experience

chunks.text: To begin with, I clearly remember getting completely lost in Beijing,
because I was wandering through an unfamiliar (不熟悉的) neighborhood.
Actually, I was trying to find a famous restaurant
but suddenly felt extremely disoriented (迷失方向的).
Because the tiny streets looked identical,
I started to go around in circles (原地打转 / 毫无进展).
At first, I was terribly panicked (惊慌失措的)
and thought finding my way back was a lost cause (注定失败的事 / 没救了).
Luckily, I used my compact (小巧的) and versatile (多功能的) smartphone
to ask a helpful (有帮助的) local police officer for directions.
Ultimately, I felt so relieved (如释重负的) when I finally arrived back at my hotel
safe and sound (安然无恙).

questions.question_text:
Describe a time when you were in an unfamiliar place and felt nervous. You should say:
where this place is
when you visited or first went there
what you did or saw there
and explain why this place is special to you

chunks.text: To begin with, this happened when I visited a big train station in Shanghai for the first time.
Actually, I was there to catch a train to another city for a short trip.
The station was very large and unfamiliar (不熟悉的),
so I could not find the right platform.
Honestly, I felt totally disoriented (迷失方向的) because all the signs looked the same to me.
I started to go around in circles (原地打转 / 毫无进展) and I nearly missed my train.
For example, I felt so panicked (惊慌失措的) that my hands were shaking.
In the end, I asked a station worker for help, which was really helpful (有帮助的),
which meant I finally found the correct platform just in time.
Of course, after that day, I always check the station map before I travel.

questions.question_text:
Describe a journey where everything went wrong. You should say:
when this happened
where it took place
what happened during it
and explain how you felt about this experience

chunks.text: I want to talk about a trip I took
to a small mountain town with my best friend last summer.
To begin with, we planned everything carefully,
but things went wrong very quickly.
Honestly, we missed our bus because we woke up late.
Then, my friend's compact (小巧的) power bank stopped working,
so our phones died completely.
Of course, this was a real problem because we had no maps.
I thought finding the right road was a lost cause (注定失败的事 / 没救了),
and I felt very stressed.
Furthermore, I realized how versatile (多功能的 (phone map)) my phone usually is,
because without it, we could not do anything.
Actually, a kind local person helped us find the correct bus stop.
In the end, we arrived safe and sound (安然无恙),
and I felt so relieved (如释重负的) that the terrible journey was finally over.

questions.question_text: Why do some people get lost more easily than others?

chunks.text: Generally speaking, some individuals get lost easily,
because they naturally lack a basic sense of spatial Orientation (方向感).
This is because, they completely ignore their surroundings
and rely entirely on digital Distraction (分心) like texting.
For instance, someone who never pays attention to a physical Landmark (地标)
will severely struggle to memorize any new city route.

questions.question_text: Do you think it is important to do some preparation before you travel to new places?

chunks.text: In my view, thorough preparation is absolutely vital,
as it ensures you remain safe in completely unknown environments.
The primary reason is that, doing research helps you quickly
Find one's feet (适应新环境 / 站稳脚跟) in a foreign culture.
However, some brave backpackers wildly prefer zero planning,
simply deciding to Cross that bridge when you come to it (船到桥头自然直).

questions.question_text: How can people find their way when they are lost?

chunks.text: Without a doubt, using mobile phone Navigation (导航) apps
is easily the most effective method for lost travelers.
This is largely due to the fact that,
GPS technology can pinpoint your exact global location almost instantly.
Alternatively, if they have no internet connection,
they must trust their own Intuition (直觉)
or bravely ask a local police officer.

questions.question_text: Is a paper map still necessary?

chunks.text: Admittedly, paper maps are rarely used daily,
but they remain strictly necessary for severe emergency backup situations.
The core issue is that, smart phones can easily run out of battery
or lose signal in rural mountains.
Consequently, if you hike entirely Off the beaten track (人迹罕至的),
a physical map is the only reliable survival tool.

questions.question_text: Has the reliance on GPS technology reduced our natural ability to navigate?

chunks.text: Undeniably, our heavy Reliance (依赖) on GPS has severely diminished
our brain's natural mapping abilities over time.
As people blindly follow screen directions,
they completely stop memorizing street names
or observing the actual physical world.
Therefore, if the Virtual (虚拟的) map crashes,
most young drivers are completely incapable of driving themselves back home safely.

questions.question_text: How does getting "lost" (metaphorically) in a new culture help a person grow?

chunks.text: It is widely acknowledged that, struggling in a new culture
intensely builds personal resilience (韧性) and extreme patience.
This is necessary since, navigating entirely unfamiliar social rules
forces individuals to step far outside their comfortable boundaries.
For example, accidentally ordering the wrong food
teaches travelers to embrace exciting mistakes
and ultimately become much more adaptable.

questions.question_text:
Describe a famous person you would like to meet. You should say:
who this person is
how you know them
what they do or have done
and explain why you find them memorable or important

chunks.text: To begin with, I would absolutely love to meet a highly influential (有影响力的) athlete named Yao Ming,
because he is a true basketball legend.
Actually, I first learned about this amazingly talented (有才华的) player
from watching his legendary (传奇的) NBA games on television.
If I met him, I would ask for some helpful (有帮助的) advice
on how to improve my own sports skills.
I think, despite always being in the limelight (处于聚光灯下 / 受人关注),
he remains incredibly humble (谦逊的) and firmly down to earth (接地气的 / 务实的).
Furthermore, his charismatic (有魅力的) personality makes him a perfect role model (榜样) for teenagers.
Ultimately, I would feel so relieved (如释重负的) and happy to shake his giant hand.

questions.question_text:
Describe a person you know who has a special talent. You should say:
who this person is
how you know them
what they do or have done
and explain why you find them memorable or important

chunks.text: I'd like to talk about Jay Chou,
a very famous singer and musician from Taiwan.
To begin with, he is incredibly talented (有才华的)
because he can write songs, play piano,
and sing all at the same time.
Honestly, his special talent is making music
that mixes Chinese style with modern pop sounds,
which makes him very unique.
He developed this talent by practising piano every day as a child,
even when it was difficult and tiring.
Actually, his mother pushed him to keep going,
and I think that really helped him grow.
Furthermore, he is quite humble (谦逊的) and down to earth (接地气的 / 务实的) in interviews,
which I really respect.
He is also very charismatic (有魅力的) on stage.
In the end, I believe he is truly influential (有影响力的)
because his music has touched millions of people across Asia.

questions.question_text:
Describe a historical figure you admire and would like to learn more about. You should say:
what it is
how you got it or learned about it
how you use it or why it matters
and explain why it is important to you

chunks.text: I'd like to talk about Marie Curie,
a legendary (传奇的) scientist who changed the world of science forever.
To begin with, she is famous for discovering two elements
and winning two Nobel Prizes, which is really amazing.
Actually, I first learned about her in my middle school science class,
where my teacher showed us a short video about her life.
Honestly, I felt so relieved (如释重负的) to know that even she faced many difficulties
but never gave up.
Furthermore, her story was very helpful (有帮助的) to me during my own hard times at school.
I think she is a true role model (榜样) for young women everywhere,
because she worked so hard in a world that didn't always support her.
In the end, I admire her because she was always in the limelight (处于聚光灯下 / 受人关注)
but stayed humble and focused.

questions.question_text: What are the advantages and disadvantages of being a famous child?

chunks.text: Generally speaking, famous children gain massive wealth early,
but sadly lose their normal childhood Privacy (隐私) completely.
This is because, their entire life is documented
by ruthless Paparazzi (狗仔队) everywhere they go daily.
For instance, child actors often Live in a fishbowl (毫无隐私的生活),
which inevitably causes severe anxiety
and strongly prevents them from making genuine friends.

questions.question_text: What can today's children do to become famous?

chunks.text: In my view, children today can easily achieve massive fame
by consistently posting creative videos on modern social media.
The primary reason is that, society has a heavy Reliance (依赖) on digital entertainment
and viral internet trends.
As an illustration, a kid playing guitar online can quickly become
a global Household name (家喻户晓的名字) overnight without any professional agent.

questions.question_text: Do people become famous because of their talent?

chunks.text: Without a doubt, true long-term fame requires genuine talent,
but short-term popularity is often just based on visual beauty.
This is crucial since, the media's current Orientation (方向 / 定位) strongly favors attractive individuals
who cause dramatic controversy.
Consequently, many influencers gain millions of followers solely due to a shocking Scandal (丑闻)
rather than any actual artistic ability.

questions.question_text: Should students have physical education and do sports at school?

chunks.text: Admittedly, physical education is incredibly essential,
because it strictly keeps young students highly active and physically healthy.
The core issue is that, modern schools focus too heavily on exams,
ignoring the massive mental health benefits of team sports.
Therefore, regular running or playing basketball makes a vital Contribution (贡献)
to reducing a teenager's daily academic stress.

questions.question_text: To what extent do celebrities sacrifice their privacy for their career?

chunks.text: Undeniably, celebrities sacrifice almost everything,
as the mainstream media completely destroys their ability to live peacefully.
Once they become successful,
losing their quiet personal life is simply the inevitable Price of fame (成名的代价).
For example, famous singers cannot even go to a supermarket
without aggressive fans demanding photos and signatures constantly.

questions.question_text: Do famous people have a moral responsibility to be good role models for society?

chunks.text: It is widely acknowledged that, stars have a huge duty
to behave properly, because millions of young children copy them.
This means that, exhibiting a toxic lifestyle
will permanently ruin their public Reputation (名誉)
and damage impressionable (易受影响的) fans.
For instance, an actor promoting charity work heavily inspires teenagers,
while an actor doing drugs normalizes terrible behavior.

questions.question_text:
Describe a shopping mall you often visit. You should say:
where this place is
when you visited or first went there
what you did or saw there
and explain why this place is special to you

chunks.text: To begin with, I want to talk about a bustling (熙熙攘攘的) shopping mall in the city center,
because I absolutely love going there.
Actually, I usually go there with my best friend
whenever we wildly want to shop till you drop (疯狂购物).
We mainly enjoy looking at trendy (时髦的) clothes,
even if we are just simply window shopping (只逛不买).
I think, it is incredibly convenient (方便的)
because the subway stops directly underneath the massive building.
As a strict bargain hunter (专门买便宜货的人),
I highly appreciate the vastly affordable (负担得起的) food options in the basement.
Even though it often gets quite overcrowded (拥挤的),
the incredibly charismatic (有魅力的) staff and highly influential (有影响力的) brand stores make it totally worthwhile.

questions.question_text:
Describe a street food area or night market you have visited. You should say:
where this place is
when you visited or first went there
what you did or saw there
and explain why this place is special to you

chunks.text: I'd like to talk about a night market
near a big shopping mall in Chengdu.
Actually, it is one of the most bustling (熙熙攘攘的) places I have ever been to.
To begin with, the market sells all kinds of food,
like spicy noodles, grilled meat, and sweet snacks.
Of course, everything there is very affordable (负担得起的),
so you don't need to spend a lot of money.
I went there with my best friend last summer,
and we both loved it.
Honestly, the food stalls looked very trendy (时髦的),
with pretty lights and nice decorations.
Furthermore, the location was convenient (方便的) because it was right next to a bus stop.
I think what I liked most was
that we could shop till you drop (疯狂购物)
and try so many different foods,
which made the whole evening really fun and memorable.

questions.question_text:
Describe a place in your city that has changed a lot in recent years. You should say:
where this place is
when you visited or first went there
what you did or saw there
and explain why this place is special to you

chunks.text: I want to talk about a big shopping mall
in my city called Wanda Plaza.
Honestly, it used to be a small, old market
where people sold cheap clothes and food.
To begin with, the place was always overcrowded (拥挤的) and a little dirty.
Now, it has totally changed into a modern, clean shopping centre.
Actually, many influential (有影响力的 (brands)) stores have moved in, like Nike and Zara.
For example, I remember one charismatic (有魅力的 (salesperson)) who made me want to buy everything he showed me.
Of course, I usually just do window shopping (只逛不买) because I don't have much money.
I think this change is good because the mall attracts every kind of shopper,
including the serious bargain hunter (专门买便宜货的人),
which means more people visit and the area feels much more alive.

questions.question_text: Some people believe building shopping malls isn't a good thing, what do you think?

chunks.text: Generally speaking, massive shopping malls destroy local culture,
as they aggressively force small, independent shops to close down entirely.
This is because, giant Retail (零售) chains strongly promote shallow Consumerism (消费主义)
rather than genuinely supporting the local community.
For instance, building a huge concrete plaza often ruins the historical neighborhood's visual charm
and heavily increases terrible traffic congestion.

questions.question_text: Why do people enjoy going to malls?

chunks.text: In my view, citizens visit malls primarily for entertainment,
because they offer a safe, climate-controlled environment for socializing.
The primary reason is that, many young workers desperately rely on
Retail therapy (购物疗法) to relieve their intense office stress.
As an illustration, a colorful window display will instantly
Catch one's eye (吸引眼球), making exhausted shoppers feel happy and completely relaxed.

questions.question_text: How did people pay in the past, and how do they pay now?

chunks.text: Undeniably, past generations used heavy physical cash,
whereas modern shoppers rely exclusively on scanning mobile phone payment codes.
This is largely due to the fact that,
advanced E-commerce (电子商务) platforms have completely digitized every single daily financial Transaction (交易).
For example, apps like Alipay allow young buyers to purchase expensive items instantly
without needing to carry a thick leather wallet.

questions.question_text: What are the downsides of mobile payments?

chunks.text: Admittedly, digital payments heavily encourage extremely Impulsive (冲动的) buying,
as spending invisible digital money feels entirely painless.
Furthermore, continuously linking your bank card to numerous phone applications
actively threatens your personal Privacy (隐私) and online security.
Consequently, a simple hacker attack could easily steal your passwords
and completely Break the bank (花费太高 / 倾家荡产) in mere seconds.

questions.question_text: Has the rise of e-commerce (online shopping) caused the death of local small businesses?

chunks.text: Without a doubt, online shopping has widely devastated local stores,
because internet platforms offer significantly cheaper retail prices.
This is crucial since, small family shops cannot realistically afford
the highly expensive rent required to maintain a physical street presence.
Therefore, many traditional bookstores have sadly gone bankrupt
because customers vastly prefer ordering cheaper digital copies directly to their front door.

questions.question_text: Is consumerism (the desire to buy more things) beneficial or harmful to society?

chunks.text: It is widely acknowledged that, extreme consumerism is highly harmful,
as it continuously destroys the natural fragile environment.
The core issue is that, rapidly manufacturing millions of cheap, disposable products
creates massive amounts of toxic plastic waste.
For instance, fast-fashion clothing brands have a terrible Reputation (声誉)
for severely polluting rivers just to sell slightly cheaper t-shirts.

questions.question_text:
Describe a book you read that you found useful. You should say:
when this happened
where it took place
what happened during it
and explain how you felt about this experience

chunks.text: To begin with, I will talk about an educational (教育意义的) book I read,
because it was extremely useful for my studies.
Actually, it is a famous classic (经典的) novel
that is currently very trendy (时髦的).
Although many people heavily judge a book by its cover (以貌取人),
the deep storyline here is incredibly captivating (迷人的).
I think, the detailed chapters are highly thought-provoking (发人深省的)
